senses.definition: Relating to a professor or to the rank and duties of a professor; characteristic of a professor or of professorship.
examples: She spoke in a professorial tone during the lecture.
The room had a professorial ambiance, filled with shelves and academic papers.

senses.definition: The behavior, language, or attitudes associated with professors or professorship; an intellectual or scholarly air.
examples: His professorialism sometimes made his explanations feel distant.
The lecture combined technical detail with a hint of professorialism.

senses.definition: In a manner characteristic of a professor; with a formal or scholarly tone.
examples: He spoke professorially about the theory, outlining every step.
She explained the concept professorially, using precise terminology.

senses.definition: The body of professorships; the group or status of professors within a university or department.
examples: The new policy affected both the professoriat and junior faculty.
She studied the history of the professoriat in European universities.

senses.definition: All professors in a university or department; the collective body of professors.
examples: The professoriate is advocating for longer sabbaticals.
Within the professoriate, research funding priorities vary.

senses.definition: Plural of professoRIAT; instances of professoriates within institutions.
examples: Several universities established new professoriates in the humanities.
Different departments have distinct professoriates.

senses.definition: Plural of professoriat; ranks or positions within the professorial staff.
examples: Several new professoriats were created this year.
Salary scales differ across professoriats.

senses.definition: Having characteristics of a professor; scholarly, authoritative.
examples: Her professorlike demeanor commanded the room.
He gave a professorlike answer, packed with data.

senses.definition: A rare or nonce formation; not widely recognized in standard dictionaries. may refer jokingly to a person who behaves like a professor or a young professor.
examples: The newcomer acted like a little professorling at the seminar.

senses.definition: People who hold the position of professor in a college or university.
examples: The professors lectured on ancient history.
Several professors attended the conference.

senses.definition: The position or rank of a professor; a permanent academic appointment.
examples: She was awarded a professorship in physics.
The professorship comes with research funding.

senses.definition: Multiple professorships; several professor positions within institutions.
examples: Several new professorships were funded this year.
Professorships in engineering have grown rapidly.

senses.definition: Relating to a professor; a professory feature or attribute; could be archaic or rare.
examples: The profresory tone crept into his lecture.

senses.definition: To offer or present something for acceptance; to put forward.
examples: She proffered her hand in greeting.
He proffered a solution to the problem.

senses.definition: An offer or proposal; something presented for acceptance.
examples: The proposal was accepted after careful consideration of the proffer.
She rejected his proffer of employment.

senses.definition: Offered or presented for acceptance.
examples: She proffered her card for the payment.
They proffered a compromise.

senses.definition: Someone who proffers something; a person who offers.
examples: The profferer explained the terms clearly.
The profferer of aid declined to reveal details.

senses.definition: Multiple proffers; people who offer something.
examples: The profferers of support gathered outside the hall.

senses.definition: The act of offering or presenting something.
examples: She was proffering assistance to the injured.
He stood there, proffering his proposal.

senses.definition: Offers or proposals; items presented for acceptance.
examples: The proffers were carefully reviewed by the committee.

senses.definition: Third-person singular present of proffer; offers or presents.
examples: The committee proffers a detailed plan.
She proffers assistance whenever possible.

senses.definition: A term that may refer to a variety of a traditional Italian snack or a regional name; not common in general English.
examples: He bought a package of profichi as a snack during the trip.

senses.definition: A rarely used synonym for proficiency or skill; literary/archaic form.
examples: Her proficience in languages impressed the teachers.

senses.definition: Ways in which someone is proficient; skills or areas of expertise.
examples: Her proficiencies include programming and data analysis.

senses.definition: Plurals of proficiency; high levels of ability in specific areas.
examples: Language proficiencies are listed on the resume.

senses.definition: A high level of ability or skill in a particular area.
examples: Her proficiency in French helped her study abroad.
Typing is a skill that requires proficiency.

senses.definition: Having a high level of skill or competence; able to do something well.
examples: She is proficient in several programming languages.
A proficient swimmer can handle complex moves.
